Strength: Turns big ideas into a shared plan that people can understand and follow.
Weakness: Can spend too much time combining ideas and keeping everyone aligned, which slows tough decisions.
Stress_Trigger: Too many priorities from different people
Breaking_point: Decision speed (keeps seeking alignment after it stops being useful)

Strength: Creates clarity early and turns unclear ideas into clear priorities and standards.
Weakness: May end exploration too early and cause pushback if others feel pushed aside.

Strength: Builds momentum through experimentation and social energy.
Weakness: Follow-through can weaken when the excitement fades and there is no outside structure.
Breaking_point: Consistency (starts many things but finishes unevenly)

Strength: Challenges assumptions quickly and turns uncertainty into learning through action.
Weakness: Can create confusion if direction changes too often.
Breaking_point: Shared direction (frequent pivots make it hard for others to stay aligned)

Strength: Creates steady progress through clear plans and support from others.
Weakness: May choose safer options instead of bigger opportunities.
Breaking_point: Ambition (shrinks the goal to keep things stable)

Strength: Performs well under pressure by setting priorities, assigning work, and enforcing standards.
Weakness: Relationships can suffer if pressure turns into constant criticism.

Strength: Keeps progress going during change through flexible coordination.
Weakness: Can take on too much responsibility and end up absorbing other people's problems.

Strength: Cuts through chaos by identifying what matters most and adapting quickly.
Weakness: Long-term systems can be neglected when urgency becomes the normal mode.
Breaking_point: Long-term planning (falls into constant emergency mode)

Strength: Keeps long-term direction through disciplined planning connected to personal values.
Weakness: Can delay action by spending too much time preparing.
Stress_Trigger: Noisy inputs and constant context switching

Strength: Builds strong systems and frameworks that stand up to careful review.
Weakness: If they do not explain their thinking clearly, others may misunderstand them.
Breaking_point: Collaboration flow (pulls back instead of explaining ideas)

Strength: Connects very different ideas into clear insight without needing attention.
Weakness: Lack of structure can stop good insight from becoming real results.
Stress_Trigger: Tight deadlines and pressure to be overly specific
Breaking_point: Shipping the work (keeps refining instead of finishing)

Strength: Explores difficult problems alone and learns quickly.
Weakness: Working too independently can make teamwork harder.
Breaking_point: Team predictability (pulls away into solo work)

Strength: Builds trust through reliability, consistency, and steady relationships.
Weakness: May avoid difficult conversations for too long to keep the peace.
Breaking_point: Truth-telling (smooths things over until problems finally erupt)

Strength: Protects standards and spots risks before things fail.
Weakness: Too much scrutiny can slow progress.

Strength: Keeps things stable through calm responsiveness and quiet problem-solving.
Weakness: Can fade into the background if they do not set their own priorities.
Breaking_point: Personal priorities (their work gets pulled apart by other people's needs)

Strength: Sees what will work right now and says it clearly.
Weakness: May overlook inspiration and the need to repair relationships.
Breaking_point: Influence (tells the truth but does not bring others along)
